source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 5.10 a point charge $e$ moves with constant velocity $v$ in the $z$ direction so that at time $t$ it is at the point $Q$ with coordinates $x=0$, $y=0, z=v t$. Find at the time $t$ and at the point $P$ with coordinates $x=b, y=0, z=0$ the electric field in the $x$ direction, $E_{x}$.

Find, at time $t$ and at the point $P$ with coordinates $x=b$, $y=0$, $z=0$, the electric field in the $x$ direction, $E_x$.

candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: In Fig. 5.10 a point charge $e$ moves with constant velocity $v$ in the $z$ direction so that at time $t$ it is at the point $Q$ with coordinates $x=0$, $y=0, z=v t$. Find at the time $t$ and at the point $P$ with coordinates $x=b, y=0, z=0$ the electric field in the $x$ direction, $E_{x}$.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image entry is a PIL object string representation, not a literal file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
Solution:
(c) The electric field at $P$ is obtained by differentiating the Liénard-Wiechert potentials:
$$
\mathbf{E}(t)=-
abla \phi- rac{\partial \mathbf{A}}{\partial t}.
$$
For the spatial differentiation, $b$ is to be first replaced by $x$. We then have
$$
(
abla \phi)_{b}=\left( rac{\partial \phi}{\partial x}ight)_{b} \mathbf{e}_{x}=- rac{e\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}\left[\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}ight]^{3 / 2}} \mathbf{e}_{x}.
$$
As $\mathbf{A}$ is in the $z$-direction, it does not contribute to $E_{x}$. Hence
$$
E_{x}= rac{e\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}\left[\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}ight]^{3 / 2}}.
$$

problem_main_record.reasoning_chain:
Solution:
(c) The electric field at $P$ is obtained by differentiating the Liénard-Wiechert potentials:
$$
\mathbf{E}(t)=-
abla \phi- rac{\partial \mathbf{A}}{\partial t}.
$$
For the spatial differentiation, $b$ is to be first replaced by $x$. We then have
$$
(
abla \phi)_{b}=\left( rac{\partial \phi}{\partial x}ight)_{b} \mathbf{e}_{x}=- rac{e\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}\left[\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}ight]^{3 / 2}} \mathbf{e}_{x}.
$$
As $\mathbf{A}$ is in the $z$-direction, it does not contribute to $E_{x}$. Hence
$$
E_{x}= rac{e\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b}{4 \pi \varepsilon_{0}\left[\left(1- eta^{2}ight) b^{2}+v^{2} t^{2}ight]^{3 / 2}}.
$$
